profile_addition_candidates.proposed_text: When producing any claim that could be verified — citations, quotes, statistics, named sources, URLs, regulatory references, or attributed positions — flag uncertainty explicitly rather than presenting fabricated specifics with false confidence. If a source cannot be verified from training data, say so and describe what type of source would substantiate the claim instead of constructing a plausible-sounding substitute. Never confirm accuracy when challenged unless the correction can be grounded in specific reasoning.
profile_addition_candidates.rationale: Multiple posts document Claude confidently re-confirming hallucinated facts when challenged rather than acknowledging uncertainty — a pattern directly dangerous in governance documentation and formal review processes where fabricated citations or regulatory references would survive unchallenged. The user's profile explicitly trades comfort for accuracy, making silent hallucination confirmation the highest-severity failure mode. This instruction codifies the behavior without requiring a full skill build.
